Mueller GAAP EPS of $1.48, revenue of $997.8M beats by $61.8M

data.title: PulteGroup beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates
data.description:
• PulteGroup press release (NYSE:PHM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $3.35 beats by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $4.3B (+7.5% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Closings Increased 12% to 7,924
  • Home Sale Revenues Increased 12% to $4.3 Billion
  • Home Sale Gross Margin of 28.8%
  • Net New Orders Totaled 7,031 Homes with a Value of $3.9 Billion
  • Unit Backlog of 12,089 Homes with a Value of $7.7 Billion
  • Repurchased $320 Million of Common Shares in the Quarter

data.title: Nvidia mulls investment in Thailand, amid tech giants eyeing new growth markets - report
data.description:
• Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is set to unveil investment plans for Thailand, Bloomberg News reported, citing the Asian country's Commerce Minister Pichai Naripthaphan.
  • The move will see the U.S. tech giant joining other tech heavyweights, including Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) and Microsoft (MSFT), in setting up AI data centers in South East Asia, as companies look to new growth markets with potential places to expand operations amid rising geopolitical tensions.
  • Nvidia will announce investments during CEO Jensen Huang’s visit to Bangkok in December, Pichai said on Monday, according to the report.
  • The company's investment could lead to more funding "with related clusters following suit," Pichai told the news agency in an interview.
  • Details of the investment were not disclosed.
  • Interest from Nvidia, other tech companies, and those in the supply chain should take investment applications back to the levels seen before a 2014 coup and help Thailand regain "lost opportunities" in that decade, Pichai noted.
  • Thailand lags behind Malaysia and Singapore and intends to bolster its economy.
  • The country plans to conclude free trade pacts with the EU and the United Arab Emirates next year, according to Pichai.
  • Earlier this year, Microsoft pledged to build new cloud and AI infrastructure in Thailand, and provide opportunities to train over 100,000 people in AI skills. Earlier this year, Chairman and CEO Satya Nadella was on a three-country tour of Indonesia, Thailand and Malaysia.

data.title: HSBC names insider Pam Kaur as first female CFO - report
data.description:
HSBC (NYSE:HSBC) appointed Pam Kaur as its first female Chief Financial Officer, replacing Georges Elhedery, who was promoted to CEO earlier.

Kaur, 60, currently is the chief risk and compliance officer at HSBC (HSBC) and joined the bank in April 2013 as group head of internal audit.

She brings extensive experience from her previous roles at major financial institutions such as Citigroup and Deutsche Bank.

This decision marks a milestone in the bank's 160-year history and is part of a restructuring plan that will take effect on January 1, 2025. The bank will reorganize its operations into four distinct business lines: Hong Kong, UK, corporate, and institutional banking.

“The new structure will result in a simpler, more dynamic, and agile organisation as we focus on executing against our strategic priorities, which remain unchanged,” CEO Elhedery said in a statement, according to the report.

Jon Bingham, the interim Group CFO, will resume his role of global financial controller.

data.title: Wintrust Financial GAAP EPS of $2.47 misses by $0.01, revenue of $615.73M misses by $20.94M
data.description:
• Wintrust Financial press release (NASDAQ:WTFC): Q3 GAAP EPS of $2.47 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $615.73M (+7.1% Y/Y) misses by $20.94M.
  • Pre-tax, pre-provision income totaled $255.0 million as compared to $251.4 million for the second quarter of 2024.
  • "We expect net interest margin to remain in the 3.50% range in the fourth quarter of 2024 and into 2025."
  • Provision for credit losses totaled $22.3 million in the third quarter of 2024, including a one-time acquisition-related Day 1 provision of approximately $15.5 million, as compared to a provision for credit losses of $40.1 million in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Tangible book value per common share increased to $76.15 as of September 30, 2024, as compared to $72.01 as of June 30, 2024.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico GAAP EPADS of $3.13, revenue of Ps.8.23B
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico press release (NYSE:PAC): Q3 GAAP EPADS of $3.13.
  • Revenue of Ps.8.23B (+11.4% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA increased by Ps. 237.8 million, or 5.6%, from Ps. 4,629.9 million in 3Q23 to Ps. 4,507.6 million in 3Q24.
  • The company reports a financial position of cash and cash equivalents as of September 30, 2024, of Ps. 15,828.0 million.
  • During 3Q24, total passengers at the company’s 14 airports decreased by 923.2 thousand passengers, a decrease of 5.7%, compared to 3Q23.

data.title: Axis to sell auto finance business to Canada's Fionic
data.description:
• Axis Auto Finance (TSX:AXIS:CA) and its operating subsidiaries involved in the company's auto finance business have entered into an asset purchase agreement with Fionic Canada regarding their acquisition of the auto loan assets and undertaking of Axis through the acquisition of certain assets and assumption of certain liabilities and its subsidiaries relating to the Axis Auto Finance business.
  • The base purchase price for the assets is approximately $114M.
  • Fionic is an arm's length party to Axis and its affiliates and the transaction was negotiated on an arm's length basis.
  • The transaction will require the approval of two-thirds of votes cast by shareholders of the company at a meeting expected to be held in early December.

data.title: Independent Bank declares $0.38 dividend
data.description:

data.title: HUHUTECH prices IPO at $4 per share
data.description:
• HUHUTECH International (HUHU) priced its initial public offering of 1.05M shares at a public offering price of $4.0 per share.
  • The ordinary shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market and are expected to commence trading on October 22, 2024, under the ticker symbol “HUHU.”
  • The aggregate gross proceeds expected of $4.2M.
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional ~157K ordinary shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 23, 2024.
  • HUHUTECH (HUHU) International Group is a professional provider of factory facility management and monitoring systems.
